summary: Large decompilation PR that replaced asm/melee/ft/ftcommon.s with src/melee/ft/ftcommon.c and wired the build to compile the C object. The PR body says ftcommon.c was matched by rtburns and snuffysasa resolved fighter merge conflicts. Supporting changes refined Fighter/ftCommonData layout, input-field accesses, function prototypes, item IDs for beam sword/parasol, JObj inline behavior, and renamed func_8007E994 to ftGetParasolStatus at call sites.
what_changed: Deleted the 4610-line asm/melee/ft/ftcommon.s and added a 2101-line src/melee/ft/ftcommon.c implementation.
Changed obj_files.mk so ftcommon is built from src/melee/ft/ftcommon.c.o instead of asm/melee/ft/ftcommon.s.o.

Added src/melee/ft/ftcommon.h with many prototypes for the 0x8007C930-0x800804FC ftcommon range, including ftGetParasolStatus, Fighter_GetModelScale, ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed, movement helpers, accessory-item helpers, and input/common-state helpers.
Renamed func_8007E994 to ftGetParasolStatus in include/functions.h, assembly callsites in code_800877F8.s, and C callsites in fighter.c.

Expanded and corrected Fighter-related structs in fighter.h, especially ftCommonData offsets and types, CollData fields, ftData nested structures, and the Fighter x20A0 field renamed to x20A0_accessory.

Updated many fighter input accesses from flat fields like x620_lstick_x and x624_lstick_y to nested input fields such as fighter->input.x620_lstick_x and fighter->input.x624_lstick_y across fighter.c, Mars special files, ftlib.c, ftwalljump.c, and ftcliffcommon.c.
Added named item enum entries It_Kind_BeamSword = 0x0C and It_Kind_Parasol = 0x0D in src/melee/it/id.h, supporting parasol-related ftcommon code.
Adjusted inline helper codegen-sensitive functions: fabs_inline was rewritten from a ternary to an if/else, and HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty now uses a local BOOL result and explicit TRUE/FALSE assignment.

Added or corrected several external prototypes in include/functions.h, including callback-typed func_8007E2D0, typed func_8007E2F4, func_8007FC7C, func_8007FE84, BOOL returns for func_8007CF58 and func_8007FFD8, and item/fighter helpers.

Kept two sandbag special-attribute accessors, func_8007CDA4 and func_8007CDF8, as inline asm in matching builds with C equivalents under NON_MATCHING because the matching issue was tied to assert/string literal emission.
smart_moves: Separated the matched implementation into a dedicated ftcommon.h instead of only relying on include/functions.h, making future ftcommon users less dependent on the global catch-all header.
Used a targeted semantic rename, ftGetParasolStatus, and propagated it to assembly call comments and C callers, improving searchability while preserving address-based function names elsewhere.

Refined struct layout only where needed for the match: ftCommonData offsets, nested input fields, accessory JObj naming, CollData offsets, and selected special-attributes fillers were adjusted to support the decompiled code.

Preserved exact matching for problematic assert-bearing functions by using inline asm under the normal matching path and C under NON_MATCHING, rather than blocking the whole file on two small functions.
Rewrote small inline helpers into more compiler-friendly shapes, such as fabs_inline if/else and HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty with an explicit local result, indicating careful attention to generated code.
Introduced named item constants for beam sword/parasol at the point where ftcommon needed readable item-kind checks.
decomp_lessons: For broad fighter-common decompilation, expect matching to require cross-file cleanup: build object lists, common headers, fighter struct offsets, item IDs, and existing character modules may all need synchronized updates.
Nested struct refactors, like moving x620_lstick_x and related fields under fighter->input, should be propagated consistently through all callsites to avoid mixing old and new layouts.

When an inline helper appears trivial, its expression form can still matter for matching. The fabs_inline ternary-to-if/else rewrite and JObj dirty check rewrite are evidence that small source-shape changes can affect codegen.
Named constants and semantic function names should be introduced when behavior is understood enough to support matching and future work, as with It_Kind_Parasol and ftGetParasolStatus.

If only a small number of functions resist matching due to assert/string literal behavior, inline asm guarded by NON_MATCHING can let the rest of a file land as C while preserving a readable fallback.
ftCommonData should retain explicit offset comments and filler ranges; this PR depended heavily on offset-accurate fields such as x164, x1FC, x454-x460, x6F4-x710, and x7B8-x7C0.
naming_conventions: Use ftGetParasolStatus for the former func_8007E994 when the function queries a fighter object's parasol state.
Use It_Kind_BeamSword and It_Kind_Parasol for item kind enum entries at 0x0C and 0x0D.
Use Fighter field name x20A0_accessory for the accessory HSD_JObj pointer instead of the raw x20A0 name.
Use fighter->input.x620_lstick_x, fighter->input.x624_lstick_y, fighter->input.x638_lsubStick_x, fighter->input.x63C_lsubStick_y, and related nested input fields rather than flat Fighter members.

Keep ftCommonData fields offset-prefixed, such as x8_someStickThreshold, x98_shieldHealthInit, x194_unkHitLagFrames, x204_knockbackFrameDecay, and x498_ledgeCooldownTime, when semantic names are only partially known.
Function names remained mostly address-based func_8007.... except where behavior was clear enough for ftGetParasolStatus, Fighter_GetModelScale, and ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced ftcommon.s with C and updated obj_files.mk to ensure the source object occupies the original assembly object's position.
Used inline asm for func_8007CDA4 and func_8007CDF8 in the matching build path, with C implementations behind NON_MATCHING.

Added custom assert_line handling with explicit lbl_803C0D58 = "ftcommon.c" and lbl_803C0D64 = "fp->kind == Ft_Kind_Sandbag" because the author noted uncertainty about why the string literal placement was needed.
Used #pragma peephole on around the inline asm/C boundary in ftcommon.c.
Adjusted fabs_inline from a ternary expression to an if/else to influence generated float absolute-value comparisons.
Adjusted HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty to compute a BOOL result through explicit conditional assignment rather than directly returning the boolean expression.
Kept assembly references in code_800877F8.s synchronized with the new symbol name ftGetParasolStatus without changing instruction bytes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body credits rtburns for matching ftcommon.c and says snuffysasa resolved merge conflicts with fighter; there is no reviewer rationale beyond that.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that removed the inline asm for func_8007CDA4 and func_8007CDF8 or fixed the assert string literal matching issue.
Search for later renames of ftcommon address-based functions added in src/melee/ft/ftcommon.h.
Check subsequent fighter.h struct-layout PRs for further ftCommonData field names and input struct stabilization.
Look for later cleanup of duplicate HSD_JObjReqAnimAll declarations in jobj.h.
Search for later item enum expansion around It_Kind_BeamSword and It_Kind_Parasol.
